_help: Copy this folder to .github/copilot/evals/<YourName>/ and replace every <...> placeholder. evals.json is REQUIRED per subject under test. Keys prefixed with _ are comments and are ignored by the tooling. Glob patterns match against audit.log paths; '*' matches any run in a case's audit.log. Delete assertion entries you don't need.
subject: <what you are testing: a prompt-file name (e.g. /migrator) or a custom agent (e.g. @audit)>
source_under_test: <the legacy project directory the agent reads, e.g. legacy-app/>

evals.name: <short name for this case>
evals.task: <the exact prompt you send the agent to produce the artifact, e.g. '@audit Audit legacy-app/ and produce docs/INVENTORY.md'>
evals.artifact_hint: <path the agent should produce, e.g. docs/PLAN.md — pass this to judge_eval.py --artifact after the run>
evals._assertions_help: Natural-language claims about the PRODUCED ARTIFACT, graded by the copilot -p LLM judge (judge_eval.py). Each becomes pass/fail.
assertions: <a claim the produced document must satisfy, e.g. 'Lists every source component that needs migrating'>
evals._knowledge_assertions_help: Optional. Specific factual claims tied to the real source code — grounds the judge in ground truth so it can't be fooled by plausible prose.

knowledge_assertions.claim: <a specific fact the artifact must reflect, e.g. 'Flags that OrderAction instantiates its service with new()'>
knowledge_assertions.source: <file (and line) in the source that grounds it, e.g. legacy-app/.../OrderAction.java:22>
knowledge_assertions.rationale: <why it matters for a correct migration>

evals._process_assertions_help: Ordering/presence checks over audit.log (process_assert.py). type=order|present|absent. Patterns are '<EVENT> <path-glob>' where EVENT is READ|WRITE|SHELL|SKILL|STEERING|*.

process_assertions.description: <source project is read-only — no writes to it>
<source is read before the output is written>

evals._context_assertions_help: Which files the agent read / which instructions were active (context_assert.py). required_steering matches STEERING entries (your .github/instructions files).
